libs_implemented: permutation_entropy (Bandt-Pompe ordinal-pattern Shannon entropy, numpy), sample_entropy (Richman-Moorman SampEn m,r, numpy), causal rolling versions of both; no pywt/ripser/iisignature/statsmodels required
notes: Permutation entropy (Bandt-Pompe) + sample entropy (Richman-Moorman) implemented in numpy as a predictability-regime gate. Tested as OVERLAY on three base books (long-BTC, x-sec TS-momentum, funding-carry) and STANDALONE. Best overlay='fund_carry': un-gated OOS Sharpe=0.85 -> gated 0.86 (Delta+0.01), gated PSR=0.877. Standalone entropy-trend OOS Sharpe=1.11 PSR=0.934 (best of 36 IS-selected variants, a directional BTC-trend = a DEAD prior here). n_variants_tried=172, within-family DSR SR*_pp=0.0694; standalone OOS SR_pp=0.0584 (FAILS deflation), best-overlay gated SR_pp=0.0448 (FAILS deflation). The only 'improving' overlay (fund_carry, Delta+0.01) raises turnover 0.0015->0.0537 (~35x) for ~zero Sharpe gain -> the gate destroys, not improves. The gate HURT long_btc and ts_mom OOS badly (IS-selected gates that flip sign OOS = textbook overfit). PRIOR check: the gate cannot create directional alpha that the dead base books lack; it only filters when to be exposed. The entropy gate fires in calm/structured regimes which overlaps trivially with realized-vol gating, so most apparent benefit is generic vol-state timing, not a unique ordinal-pattern edge. close-to-close maxDD is an illusion (no intrabar liq/gap).
data_caveats: daily close-to-close perp returns 2022-2026; entropy is a causal trailing-window statistic (no look-ahead, gate shifted 1 bar); threshold quantile fit on TRAIN only. maxDD close-to-close (no intrabar liq/gap). MATIC/NEAR/ATOM excluded (no 1d fut cache). Sample-entropy rolling uses step=3 ffill for speed (still causal). PE-gate regime overlaps heavily with realized-vol regime.
